s2.ptxt: Use the removed magnets to collect any steel chips. Examine the chips and particles in the pan and on the magnets to determine what type of wear has occurred in the transaxle:
Using a dial indicator, measure the input shaft end play.
Check if the sliding surfaces of the discs, plates, and flange are worn or burnt. If necessary, replace them.

atten3.ptxt: If the linings of the discs are peeled off or discolored, or even if a part of the groove is damaged, replace all discs.

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, measure the inside diameter of the bushings of the planetary sun gear.
If the inside diameter is greater than the maximum, replace the planetary gear sun gear.
Check if the sliding surfaces of the discs, plates, and flange are worn or burnt. If necessary, replace them.

atten3.ptxt: If the linings of the discs are peeled off or discolored, or even if a part of the groove is damaged, replace all discs.

s2.ptxt: Check if the sliding surfaces of the discs, plates, and flange are worn or burnt. If necessary, replace them.

atten3.ptxt: If the linings of the discs are peeled off or discolored, or even if a part of the groove is damaged, replace all discs.

s2.ptxt: Using a feeler gauge, measure the clearance between the underdrive planetary gear and the pinion gear at 4 points.
If the clearance is greater than the standard clearance, replace the underdrive planetary gear sun gear.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT REAR PLANETARY SUN GEAR ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, measure the inside diameter of the bushing of the rear planetary sun gear sub-assembly.
If the inside diameter is greater than the maximum, replace the rear planetary sun gear sub-assembly.
Check if the sliding surfaces of the discs, plates, and flange are worn or burnt. If necessary, replace them.

atten3.ptxt: If the linings of the discs are peeled off or discolored, or even if a part of the groove is damaged, replace all discs.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT 2ND BRAKE PISTON RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Using vernier calipers, measure the free length of the 3 2nd brake piston return springs including the spring seats.
If the free length is shorter than the standard free length, replace the 2nd brake piston return spring.
Temporarily install the one-way clutch assembly to the planetary ring gear.
Rotate the one-way clutch assembly to check the rotating direction for lock or free operation.
If the one-way clutch assembly does not operate normally, replace it.
Using a feeler gauge, measure the clearance between the planetary gear case and each pinion gear at points A and B.
If the clearance is greater than the standard clearance, replace the planetary gear assembly.
Check if the sliding surfaces of the discs, plates, and flange are worn or burnt. If necessary, replace them.

atten3.ptxt: If the linings of the discs are peeled off or discolored, or even if a part of the groove is damaged, replace all discs.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT 1ST AND REVERSE BRAKE RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Using vernier calipers, measure the free length of the 1st and reverse brake return spring together with the spring seat.
If the free length is shorter than the standard free length, replace the 1st and reverse brake return spring.
Using a dial indicator, measure the clearance of the No. 2 brake while applying compressed air (200 kPa, 2.0 kgf/cm2, 29 psi).

atten4.ptxt: Measure the clearance at 3 points where the brake piston diameter is approximately 140 mm (5.51 in.) and calculate the average.

s2.ptxt: If the clearance is not as specified, select an appropriate No. 2 brake flange so that the clearance will be within the specified range.

atten4.ptxt: There are 5 different thicknesses of flanges available.

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, measure the clearance of the No. 1 brake while applying compressed air (200 kPa, 2.0 kgf/cm2, 29 psi).

atten4.ptxt: Measure the clearance at 3 points where the brake piston diameter is approximately 140 mm (5.51 in.) and calculate the average.

s2.ptxt: If the clearance is not as specified, select an appropriate No. 1 brake flange so that the clearance will be within the specified range.

atten4.ptxt: There are 6 different thicknesses of flanges available.

s2.ptxt: Using vernier calipers and a straight edge, measure the distance shown in the illustration (Dimension A) while a load of 500 N (51 kgf, 112 lbf) is being applied to the flange.

atten4.ptxt: Measure dimension A at 3 points where the flange diameter is approximately 166 mm (6.54 in.) and calculate the average.

s2.ptxt: Using vernier calipers and a straight edge, measure the distance shown in the illustration. (Dimension B).

atten4.ptxt: Measure dimension B at 3 points where the No. 3 brake diameter is approximately 166 mm (6.54 in.) and calculate the average.

s2.ptxt: Calculate the clearance value using the following formula:
clearance = Dimension B - Dimension A
If the clearance is not as specified, select an appropriate brake flange so that the clearance will be within the specified range.

atten4.ptxt: There are 6 different thicknesses of flanges available.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT CLEARANCE OF NO. 1 CLUTCH DISC

s2.ptxt: Install the direct multiple disc clutch assembly onto the transaxle rear cover.
Using a dial indicator, measure the No. 1 clutch pack clearance while applying and releasing compressed air (200 kPa, 2.0 kgf/cm2, 29 psi).

atten4.ptxt: Measure the clearance at 3 points where the flange diameter is approximately 152 mm (5.98 in.) and calculate the average.

s2.ptxt: If the pack clearance is not as specified, inspect the discs, plates and flange.

atten4.ptxt: There are 6 different thicknesses of flanges available.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT CLEARANCE OF NO. 2 CLUTCH DISC

s2.ptxt: Install the direct multiple disc clutch assembly onto the transaxle rear cover.
Using a dial indicator, measure the No. 2 clutch pack clearance while applying and releasing compressed air (200 kPa, 2.0 kgf/cm2, 29 psi).

atten4.ptxt: Measure the clearance at 3 points where the diameter of the No. 2 direct multiple clutch piston is approximately 152 mm (5.98 in.) and calculate the average.

s2.ptxt: If the pack clearance is not as specified, inspect the discs, plates and flange.

atten4.ptxt: There are 6 different thicknesses of flanges available.
